mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contractions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging trends between housing starts and consumer credit growth indicate conflicting demand signals that increase market uncertainty and volatility, reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth deceleration combined with elevated consumer credit levels signals a demand-driven credit cycle peak that triggers equity valuation compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with peak housing starts
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when the trigger activates, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for market beta and volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding corporate capacity utilization and healthy aggregate supply, which supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.0 percent per annum tightens financial conditions sufficiently to suppress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when the threshold is breached, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in a rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and rising consumer price inflation indicate an overheating labor market that forces aggressive monetary tightening, ultimately depressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in the majority of observations, or the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate return model.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing labor force expansion signals rising productivity gains that lower unit labor costs and boost corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change exceeds PAYEMS month-over-month change
failure_condition: The productivity spread shows no correlation with forward equity returns, or the signal generates negative returns when the trigger activates.
